_cite.reason: Span re-point (item 3): fact_span_not_in_source FACT whose span is verbatim in another pool source — re-point search_result_id/source_id/tier to the source that contains it. Deterministic, no LLM. Operator ruling 2026-07-04.

prior.claim_text: In December 2025, the Commission presented the Automotive Package, which proposes a targeted amendment to the existing CO₂ emission standards for HDVs. The proposed amendment allows manufacturers to collect more emission credits in the years before 2030 than under the current Regulation, specifically by enabling credit generation as soon as emissions fall below the annual emission target (rather than only when below the linear emissions reduction trajectory).
prior.source_span: In December 2025 , the Commission presented the Automotive Package , which set an ambitious yet pragmatic policy framework to ensure climate neutrality by 2050 and strategic independence while providing more flexibility to manufacturers. The Package proposes a targeted amendment to the existing CO 2 emission standards for HDVs , aiming to provide manufacturers with an additional flexibility to ease the compliance with their applicable 2030 CO 2 emissions targets. The proposed targeted amendment allows manufacturers to collect more emission credits in the years before 2030 than in the current Regulation. While the Regulation (EU) 2019/1242 on CO 2 emission standards for HDVs currently allows manufacturers to gain credits only when their CO 2 emissions are below a linear emissions reduction trajectory, with the proposal they would be able to generate credits as soon as their CO 2 emissions are below their annual emission target.
